mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the 0.2pp threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which expands corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when industrial production declines during high inflation periods, or the signal shows no predictive power in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 3.5% while the federal funds rate stays below 5.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition fails to predict negative returns, with a positive hit rate exceeding 50% over a 4-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show a negative correlation with payroll growth, or the Rank IC falls below -0.02 across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP coinciding with falling retail sales confirms a broad demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the predictive accuracy does not significantly outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which boosts near-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following industrial production increases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 in out-of-sample testing.
